borrowed: Treat post-live support and service-management obligations as part of the integrated route lifecycle.
Keep limited-release and full-release context visible to operators.
rejected: Do not mirror public guidance page layout inside the internal operator shell.

borrowed: WCAG 2.2 AA evidence is treated as an integration requirement.
Clinical safety, data privacy, and SCAL evidence are kept as first-class refs.
rejected: Do not expose raw identity assertions or patient identifiers in support tables.

borrowed: Plain operational explanation and inclusive accessibility responsibilities.
Calm, low-noise state copy for patient-visible summaries.
rejected: Do not use marketing-style page sections or oversized explanatory cards.

borrowed: Each browser proof creates an isolated context.
URL state is asserted after interaction rather than relying on test order.
rejected: Do not share browser storage between the four proof specs.

borrowed: Combine automated browser assertions with manual-review notes.
Use role-aware checks for headings, tabs, links, and focus.
rejected: Do not claim automated checks replace full manual accessibility assessment.

borrowed: Browser proof can preserve screenshots and action context for debugging.
The visual spec writes a deterministic screenshot artifact.
rejected: Do not depend on trace upload services for local proof.

borrowed: Use native buttons with `role=tab` and selected state for inspector section selection.
rejected: Do not replace native buttons with div-based controls.

borrowed: Keep dock/stack inspector controls explicit and keyboard reachable.
rejected: Do not hide critical freeze state in collapsed-only content.

borrowed: Prefer purpose-built, clear, low-busy-work operational surfaces.
Use one vocabulary for the work instead of invented parallel labels.
rejected: Do not make the workbench look like an issue backlog.

borrowed: Persist deep state in the URL.
Use clear focus rings, keyboard-operable flows, and reduced-motion variants.
rejected: Do not add ornamental section chrome that competes with the channel facts.

borrowed: Use native tables for dense disclosure-safe audit fields.
Give dense data enough width and avoid nested tables.
rejected: Do not add spreadsheet-like editing controls to audit rows.
